rationale: In the citing paper, Zhu, Chen, and Dasgupta (2008) is used in Study 1b as a specific methodological precedent for constructing a key measure: the reverse-coded willingness-to-spend-to-repair item, described as motivated by prior willingness-to-pay-for-upgrade measures. This is more than a generic background mention because it directly supports how the authors operationalize part of their dependent construct (product neglect). The citation is not central to the paper's main theory, but it plays a clear functional role in measurement design.
relation_description: The citing paper applies measurement logic from Zhu et al. (2008) on willingness to pay in upgrade-related decisions to its own context, using it to justify a repair-versus-replace valuation measure in Study 1b. The cited work is used as a methodological template rather than being challenged or theoretically extended.
